steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must "make a generalization." A generalization states the common pattern across sources without naming specific researchers, dates, or locations.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — states the shared conclusion without specifics: Correct. No researcher names, no dates, no locations — just the finding that applies across all sources. ✓
  • Choice B — focuses on one specific study: Wrong. Tying the claim to a single study is not a generalization. ✗
  • Choice C — lists both studies with years: Wrong. Citing specific authors and dates keeps it at the level of individual findings, not a generalization. ✗
  • Choice D — defines a term instead of stating the finding: Wrong. Defining a term sidesteps the shared conclusion entirely. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
